=LEVEL ε: Incessant Hum-Buzz= {{User:JakitoFan/Class Custom/4|class=THETA|hexagon=ϑ|header=LEVEL CLASS:|safety=Unsafe, Unstable|security=Mental and Auditory Hazards|entity=Minor Entity Presence|bg-image=Level Koppa.png|bg-size=1500px|text-color=#ffffff|chevron= Ͱ}} Level εIn plain text, this is 'Level Infinitesimal'., also known as Level Epsilon and incorrectly known as Level 0.ε, is a notable sublevel of [[Level 0]]. It was also formerly referred to as 'Level Theta'.{{Blurimage|file=Kevel Loppa.png|caption=An image of an unstable region in the level.|hovertext=The hum-buzz is maddening.}}
==Description==
Level ε is formatted similarly to Level 0, with sickening yellow walls, beige carpet and monotonous fluorescent lights. However, the layout is vastly different compared to its parent level. Typically, Level ε's rooms are far larger and less maze-like compared to its parent level's. Level ε's terrain is multi-leveled, with structural anomalies being frequent. These anomalies include things like tunnels, raised platforms, staircases to nowhere in particular and decorations being abound. |-|Structual Anomaly #1= {{Blurimage|file=Lllllkoppa.png|caption=An example image of Structural Anomaly #1.|hovertext=It sickens you.}}Structural Anomaly #1, also known as Heights, is the most well-known and commonly photographed area of Level ε. It consists one large room, with extremely high ceilings. There is no wallpaper to be found, only the yellow coloring that appears to mimic the wallpaper around it. Cracks and dust litter the walls, creating an unkempt feeling that persists alongside the occasional pieces of old and withered furniture, ranging from couches to steel chairs. Blank pieces of paper litter the floor, creating a small tripping hazard. Ceiling tiles are missing from the ceiling. The most glaring different is that two staircases on either side lead to large floors closer up to the ceiling, with at least one entrance for each floor to a different area of the level. There are also two 'ground level' entrances, which are located perpendicular to the staircases, which are built into the wall. |-|Structural Anomaly #2= {{Blurimage|file=EevelL.gif|caption=A recording of Windowrooms, showing off a small portion of the rooms.|hovertext=The buzzing hurts your brain.}}Structural Anomaly #2, also known as Windowrooms, are another relatively common area of Level ε. Once again the wallpaper is lacking, with only a handful of walls having it. The entrances to these areas have wooden doorframes far thicker than the rest, and the fluorescent lights are similarly powerful. Beige and off-white boxes line the walls. Uniquely, the walls within these areas are adorned by windows with white frames, sectioned into individual small panes separated by wooden dividers. The carpet is noticeably darker compared to areas like Heights. These areas are some of the smallest one may come across when traversing the level. |-|Structural Anomaly #3= {{Blurimage|file=IMG 0439.png|caption=A photograph of the Slopes.|hovertext=A hundred migraines rolled into one.}}Slopes are the third primary Structural Anomaly within Level ε. They manifest as deep impressions within the ground of the level, often filled with loose wires. Small walls fill the space, barely tall enough to obscure a child's vision. Shelves have a chance to line each wall, typically bare but occasionally having small amounts of supplies. Parts of the wallpaper are oversaturated, and yellowed boxes line the corner. Precariously placed vents blast piping-hot air, providing a risk of burns; the air does not seem to affect the surroundings in the slightest. Impressions are made into the walls, seemingly having no purpose. |-|Structural Anomaly #4= {{Blurimage|file=RESIDENCE.png|caption=A photograph of the Residences.|Hovertext=My head is in agony.}}Residences are the fourth primary Structural Anomaly within Level ε. They manifest as a series of walls with several gaps mimicking doorways, as well as a unique interior. The interior often holds velvet blue furniture, tables, chairs and at least one painting. These paintings manifest as green fields of grass with blue skies and white clouds. These paintings have mind to severe psychological effects on the wanderer, often making them feel disturbed and uncomfortable. They also tend to hasten the onset of Fluorescent Madness. While the Residences are comfy, due to the psychological threats it is advised to give these areas a wide berth. |-|Further Structual Anomalies= The Structural Anomalies listed above were the top three most common, listed in order of their discovery. While other structural anomalies are capable of appearing, they are difficult to locate, and most changes are minor, leaving no reasons to document them further. However, if other notable structural anomalies occur, more information will be posted. ==Associated Phenomena== The primary phenomenon within this level is Phonophobia. ===Phonophobia=== Phonophobia is a phenomenon primarily caused by the buzzing lights of the level. For unknown reasons the structure of these lights causes them to emit a concentrated frequency that enters the brain. These frequencies are inaudible but are known to cause major damage. The frequencies activate certain parts of the immune system, misidentifying parts of the brain as foreign substances and causing the immune system to attack the brain. The initial symptom of this is a moderate bout of tinnitus. The ringing noise is mostly benign but is typically an annoyance. Shortly after, the buzzing sounds will be perceived as getting louder and more invasive, which will slowly begin to impact the wanderer's mental state. ~45 minutes after the initial onset, pounding migraines will start affecting the wanderer, with them being described as painful and excruciating. These headaches will slowly build to a crescendo, at which the wanderer will pass out and fall to the floor. The frequencies will continue damaging the brain, oftentimes leading to total brain death. This leaves them as fodder for the rest of the entities. While technically curable, quick action must be taken to remove the affected individuals from the level, and a long period of recovery must take place for them to be cured. Wanderers may sometimes be resistant to the phenomenon, converting to [[Phenomenon 35|Wretches]] before the phenomenon takes full hold. For unknown reasons, upon reaching Stage IV the affected individuals will instantaneously noclip out of the level and into another, most commonly [[Level 6]]. ===Supplementary Phenomena=== *[[Phenomenon 14]]: Geometry in this level is relatively stable, but multiple sections can occupy the same space without overlap, which can be explained via Phenomenon 14. *[[Phenomenon 11]]: The level is notably unstable, on average scoring a 6.8 on the [[Phenomenon 11|Morrison Stability Scale]], being on par with levels such as [[Level 3]]. *[[Phenomenon 35]]: Individuals affected by the Fluorescent Madness have a tendency to convert to Wretches; although they noclip out of the level shortly after. *[[Phenomenon 15]]: Only highly specific locations are affected by this phenomenon; usually distinguishable via a lighter hue, and made painfully obvious when members of your group seemingly blink out of existence. *[[Phenomenon 12-A]]: While this level was only lightly affected by the Flicker, the lights still tend to go out at any given time; during this period entity activity hits an all time low. *[[Phenomenon 1-B]]: Within this level, and for ~30 minutes after exiting, one will be entirely unable to no-clip; suggesting a temporary onset of No-Clip Disorder. ==Entities== *[[Entity 2|Windows]] reside tucked away in Windowrooms, hiding amongst other inanimate windows. Give berths to suspicious looking windows. *[[Entity 63|Predatory Doors]] may occasionally manifest as partially-rotten wooden doors within the level. Give those doors berths as well. ==Entrances and Exits==
===Entrances=== *Stylistically deviant areas of [[Level 0]] have a significant chance of arriving here, *Staircases in drier sections of [[Level 0.8]] may lead here, *Typically, entrances from [[Level 0]]'s many sublevels manifest as doorways, **This also applies to [[Level 27]]. *Focusing too hard on [[Level 209]]'s ballroom music cacophony may cause you to enter this level without noticing, *And finally, unknown factors may cause interiors of buildings in [[Level 98]] to fade into this level.
===Exits=== *This level may rarely fade into [[Level 0]] or its sublevels, **[[Level 27]]'s exits operate the same way, *[[Level 810]]'s respective entrances appear in rooms bare of wallpaper, *Attempting to no-clip will almost certainly send one to [[The Void]], *Rapid increases in [[Phenomenon 11|Instability]] may send one to [[The Broken]], *No-clipping in paintings might send one to [[Level 241]] or [[Level 365]]. *And finally, red carpeting may occasionally appear, any exits taken in close proximity will send one to [[Level 959|█████ ███]].
}}
